violations.text: The following were observed in poor repair: -prep unit gaskets -prep unit handle (missing). New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
The floors throughout the facility were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

The floor and wall junctures in several areas of the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024
Ceiling tiles in the service area (over ovens) were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The floor and wall junctures in areas of the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

The floors in the kitchen area is in poor repair, with areas worn through several layers of flooring, impacting its ability to effectively be cleaned. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024
The ceiling tiles in the service area is in poor repair, observed with pieces peeling. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and a live mouse observed during inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC stated both establishment and building have routine PCS services. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The door to the outside, located at the emergency exit, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

Containers of bottled beverages were observed on the floor in the front service area where rodent activity was observed. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Inspector provided test strips during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the several refrigeration units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
The covers to the waste oil receptacle outside the back door have an accumulation of oil and oil and grease. Waste receptacles must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
The ceiling tiles in the front service area are in poor repair, observed with pieces peeling. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and/or rodent droppings: -behind chest freezer in service area -under milk crates used to store bags of flour. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The floors are in poor repair, with areas worn through several layers of previous flooring, impacting its ability to be effectively cleaned. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

The floor and wall junctures in some areas of the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Mixer is to be cleaned and sanitized before use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023
Shelves in areas of the kitchen observed not in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

The cutting boards in the prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023
The waste oil receptacle outside the back door have an accumulation of oil and oil and grease. Waste receptacles must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/03/2023

The walk in cooler is in poor repair, with gaps between ceiling panels, exposing insulation, wood, and the ceiling and/or floor of the 2nd story of building. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

The floors are in poor repair, with areas worn through several layers of previous floor, impacting its ability to be effectively cleaned. Areas of coving are in poor repair, pulling away from the surface and now accumulating food debris. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024

The floor and wall junctures in some areas of the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2024
Menu does not contain an allergen statement. English Food Allergies What You Need to Know New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
The hand-washing sink in the front service area was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
Working containers (spray bottle) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
Food observed stored on the floor in the pizza room, walk in cooler, and dough room. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Single service items observed stored on the floor beneath the ovens, in the storage area of dough room, and basement. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Exposed hardware observed on the slicer, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized, and a dial missing, replaced with a vice grip. Slicer was taken permanently out of service and must be removed from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
Shelves in areas of the kitchen observed not in good repair, with peeling laminate. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

The cutting board at the food prep unit, and stored beneath the slicer are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units, area beneath flattop grill, friers, grill, can opener base, shelves, counters, portion cup bin, walk in cooler shelves, etc. etc. have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, mold, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
The waste oil receptacles outside the back door have an accumulation of oil and grease. Waste receptacles must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

The walk in cooler is in poor repair, with gaps between ceiling panels, exposing insulation, wood chips, and the ceiling and/or floor of 2nd story of building. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2023

The floors, walls, ceilings, and ceiling light covers throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in several areas of the establishment, including the basement, where there is an accumulation of clutter. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

The floors are in poor repair, with areas worn through several layers of previous floor, impacting its ability to be effectively cleaned.Areas of coving are in poor repair, pulling away from the surface and now accumulating food debris.Trim on half wall near 3 bay sink is unattached. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The floor and wall junctures in some areas of the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
